title: Authors and Citation
text: Tanguy Barthelemy. Author, maintainer, artist.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Barthelemy T (2026). rjd3production: Prepare production seasonnal adjustment JDemetra+. R package version 1.0.0.9000, https://tanguybarthelemy.github.io/rjd3production/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rjd3production: Prepare for production of seasonnal adjustment with JDemetra+},   author = {Tanguy Barthelemy},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0.9000},   url = {https://tanguybarthelemy.github.io/rjd3production/}, }

title: Prepare for production of seasonnal adjustment with JDemetra+
text: {rjd3production} aide les producteurs de données CVS TD à mettre en place des chaînes de production. Il permet notamment de : Créer des calendriers français et régresseurs de calendrier compatibles JDemetra+ Identifier des SAI par leur nom Selectionner les jeux de calendrier pour une ou plusieurs séries Manipuler les régresseurs de calendrier et les outliers d’un Workspace selon la dynamique suivante: Les fonctions import_XXX() et export_XXX() permettent de convertir les data.frame contenant les outliers et régresseurs de calendrier en fichiers et inversement Les fonctions retrieve_XXX() et assign_XXX() permettent d’extraire (resp. d’assigner) les outliers et régresseurs de calendrier d’un workspace
code:
flowchart LR %% Objects WS["WS
(JDemetra+
workspace)"] DF_OUT["outliers_df
(data.frame)"] DF_TD["td_df
(data.frame)"] YAML_OUT["outliers YAML
(outliers_<ws_name>.yaml)"] YAML_TD["TD YAML
(td_<ws_name>.yaml)"] SERIES["Series
(time series data)"] %% Outliers workflow DF_OUT -->|"assign_outliers()"| WS WS -->|"retrieve_outliers()"| DF_OUT DF_OUT -->|"export_outliers()"| YAML_OUT YAML_OUT -->|"import_outliers()"| DF_OUT %% TD workflow SERIES -->|"select_td()"| DF_TD WS -->|"retrieve_td()"| DF_TD DF_TD -->|"export_td()"| YAML_TD YAML_TD -->|"import_td()"| DF_TD DF_TD -->|"assign_td()"| WS %% Styles classDef ws fill:#e6f2ff,stroke:#4a7ebb,stroke-width:1px; classDef df fill:#e9f7ef,stroke:#2e8b57,stroke-width:1px; classDef yaml fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#cc8400,stroke-width:1px; classDef series fill:#f5e6ff,stroke:#7a3db8,stroke-width:1px; class WS ws class DF_OUT,DF_TD df class YAML_OUT,YAML_TD yaml class SERIES series

title: Prepare for production of seasonnal adjustment with JDemetra+
text: {rjd3production} s’appuie sur le paquetage {rJava} L’exécution des paquets rjd3 nécessite Java 17 ou plus. La manière de mettre en place une telle configuration dans R est expliquée ici

title: Prepare for production of seasonnal adjustment with JDemetra+
text: Pour obtenir la version stable actuelle (à partir de la dernière version) : Depuis GitHub : De r-universe :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("TanguyBarthelemy/rjd3production@*release") install.packages("rjd3production", repos = c("https://TanguyBarthelemy.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org"))

title: Prepare for production of seasonnal adjustment with JDemetra+
text: Vous pouvez installer la version de développement de {rjd3production} depuis [GitHub] (https://github.com/) avec :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("TanguyBarthelemy/rjd3production")

title: Prepare for production of seasonnal adjustment with JDemetra+
text: {rjd3production} helps producers CVS TD data set production lines. particular, enables : Create JDemetra+-compatible French calendars calendar regressors Identify SAIs name Select calendar sets one series Manipulate Workspace calendar regressors outliers according following dynamics: import_XXX() export_XXX() functions convert data.frames containing calendar outliers regressors files, vice versa. retrieve_XXX() assign_XXX() functions extract (resp. assign) calendar outliers regressors workspace.
code:
flowchart LR %% Objects WS["WS
(JDemetra+
workspace)"] DF_OUT["outliers_df
(data.frame)"] DF_TD["td_df
(data.frame)"] YAML_OUT["outliers YAML
(outliers_<ws_name>.yaml)"] YAML_TD["TD YAML
(td_<ws_name>.yaml)"] SERIES["Series
(time series data)"] %% Outliers workflow DF_OUT -->|"assign_outliers()"| WS WS -->|"retrieve_outliers()"| DF_OUT DF_OUT -->|"export_outliers()"| YAML_OUT YAML_OUT -->|"import_outliers()"| DF_OUT %% TD workflow SERIES -->|"select_td()"| DF_TD WS -->|"retrieve_td()"| DF_TD DF_TD -->|"export_td()"| YAML_TD YAML_TD -->|"import_td()"| DF_TD DF_TD -->|"assign_td()"| WS %% Styles classDef ws fill:#e6f2ff,stroke:#4a7ebb,stroke-width:1px; classDef df fill:#e9f7ef,stroke:#2e8b57,stroke-width:1px; classDef yaml fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#cc8400,stroke-width:1px; classDef series fill:#f5e6ff,stroke:#7a3db8,stroke-width:1px; class WS ws class DF_OUT,DF_TD df class YAML_OUT,YAML_TD yaml class SERIES series

title: Prepare for production of seasonnal adjustment with JDemetra+
text: get current stable version (latest release): GitHub: r-universe:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("TanguyBarthelemy/rjd3production@*release") install.packages("rjd3production", repos = c("https://TanguyBarthelemy.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org"))

title: Prepare for production of seasonnal adjustment with JDemetra+
text: can install development version {rjd3production} GitHub :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("TanguyBarthelemy/rjd3production")

what: Assign outliers to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_outliers
title: Assign outliers to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_outliers
text: function updates JDemetra+ workspace (.xml) inserting pre-specified outliers domainSpec series (SA-Item).

title: Assign outliers to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_outliers
code: assign_outliers(jws, outliers)

title: Assign outliers to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_outliers
text: jws Java Workspace object, returned rjd3workspace::jws_open() rjd3workspace::jws_new(). outliers [list] named list element corresponds series workspace created retrieve_outliers import_outliers.

title: Assign outliers to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_outliers
text: object jws updated new pre-specified outliers.

title: Assign outliers to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_outliers
text: function modifies first SA-Processing.

title: Assign outliers to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_outliers
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Retrieve outliers from an existing workspace outs <- retrieve_outliers("workspace.xml")  # Reapply them to another workspace assign_outliers(outliers = outs, ws_path = "workspace.xml") } # }

what: Assign calendar (TD) regressors to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_td
title: Assign calendar (TD) regressors to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_td
text: function updates JDemetra+ workspace (.xml) assigning user-defined trading day regressors (TD) series (SA-Item), based external correspondence table, can created retrieve_td(). function modifies domainSpec series setting tradingdays "UserDefined" appropriate regressors (REG1 … REG6, optionally LY leap year).

title: Assign calendar (TD) regressors to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_td
code: assign_td(td, jws)

title: Assign calendar (TD) regressors to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_td
text: td [data.frame] data.frame least two columns: series: name series workspace. regs: standard INSEE TD set (REG1, REG2, …, REG6, without _LY) (created retrieve_td import_td). jws Java Workspace object, returned rjd3workspace::jws_open() rjd3workspace::jws_new().

title: Assign calendar (TD) regressors to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_td
text: object jws updated new td regressors.

title: Assign calendar (TD) regressors to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_td
text: function modifies first SA-Processing.

title: Assign calendar (TD) regressors to a JDemetra+ workspace — assign_td
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Load a workspace and apply INSEE TD sets td_table <- retrieve_td("workspace.xml") assign_td(td = td_table, ws_path = "workspace.xml") } # }

title: Compare series across workspaces — compare
text: Reads multiple JDemetra+ workspaces extracts comparable series (SAI series type), returning tidy format. particularly useful compare results across different specifications (e.g. RSA3 vs RSA5).

title: Compare series across workspaces — compare
code: compare(..., series_names)

title: Compare series across workspaces — compare
text: ... character Workspace file paths. series_names character Vector SAI names compare.

title: Compare series across workspaces — compare
text: data.frame columns: ws: workspace name (derived file basename), SAI: SAI name, series: type series, date: observation date, value: numeric value.

title: Compare series across workspaces — compare
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Two demo workspaces (RSA3 and RSA5) path1 <- file.path(tempdir(), "workspace_RSA3.xml") path2 <- file.path(tempdir(), "workspace_RSA5.xml")  df <- compare(path1, path2, series_names = "series_1") head(df) } # }

what: Creating a set of X13 specifications — create_specs_set
title: Creating a set of X13 specifications — create_specs_set
text: Builds set X13 specifications start date, modelling context (explanatory variables) outliers (optional).

title: Creating a set of X13 specifications — create_specs_set
code: create_specs_set(   spec_0 = NULL,   context = NULL,   outliers = NULL,   span_start = NULL )

title: Creating a set of X13 specifications — create_specs_set
text: spec_0 Basic specification context list Modeling context created rjd3toolkit::modelling_context(). outliers [list NULL] Optional list elements : type: vector outlier types (e.g. "AO", "LS", "TC") date: vector corresponding dates span_start character Estimation start date (format "YYYY-MM-DD").

title: Creating a set of X13 specifications — create_specs_set
text: list named X13 specifications (TD variants).

title: Creating a set of X13 specifications — create_specs_set

what: Create a Workspace from Data — create_ws_from_data
title: Create a Workspace from Data — create_ws_from_data
text: Creates new JDemetra+ workspace columns time series object using specified specification. column x interpreted separate time series added newly created Seasonal Adjustment Processing (SAP).

title: Create a Workspace from Data — create_ws_from_data
code: create_ws_from_data(x, spec = rjd3x13::x13_spec())

title: Create a Workspace from Data — create_ws_from_data
text: x time series object (e.g. ts, mts, matrix coercible ts) column represents series seasonally adjusted. Column names used SA-Item names. spec JDemetra+ specification. Defaults rjd3x13::x13_spec().

title: Create a Workspace from Data — create_ws_from_data
text: JDemetra+ workspace object (Java pointer) containing one SA-Processing one SA-Item per column x.

title: Create a Workspace from Data — create_ws_from_data
text: series share specification (spec).

title: Create a Workspace from Data — create_ws_from_data
code: data(AirPassengers)  # Create a multivariate time series x <- cbind(   series1 = AirPassengers,   series2 = log(AirPassengers) )  # Create workspace ws <- create_ws_from_data(x)

what: Diagnostics Extraction on Calendar Correction with different sets of regressors — diagnostics_selection
title: Diagnostics Extraction on Calendar Correction with different sets of regressors — diagnostics_selection
text: functions allow extract diagnostics X13-Arima models different sets calendar regressors order evaluate different specifications select appropriate calendar regressors set (without leap-year effect) correct given series.

title: Diagnostics Extraction on Calendar Correction with different sets of regressors — diagnostics_selection
code: get_LY_info(smod, verbose = TRUE)  one_diagnostic(series, spec, context)  all_diagnostics(series, specs_set, context)  verif_LY(jeu, diags)  select_reg_one_series(series, name = "", specs_set = NULL, context = NULL, ...)

title: Diagnostics Extraction on Calendar Correction with different sets of regressors — diagnostics_selection
text: smod list Result summary() applied X13 model. series [ts numeric] Time series analyse. spec list X13 specification (rjd3x13::x13_spec()). context list Modelling context regressors calendars (rjd3toolkit::modelling_context()). specs_set [list NULL] List X13 specifications. NULL, generated via create_specs_set(). jeu character Name tested regression set. diags data.frame Diagnostics table produced all_diagnostics(). name character Name series (messages). ... Additional arguments passed create_specs_set() controlling generation X13 specifications. Possible arguments include: outliers Optional list outliers elements type (vector types, e.g., "AO", "LS", "TC") date (vector dates). span_start Starting date estimation (character, format "YYYY-MM-DD"). ... arguments accepted create_specs_set().

title: Diagnostics Extraction on Calendar Correction with different sets of regressors — diagnostics_selection
text: get_LY_info() : data.frame LY_coeff LY_p_value. one_diagnostic() : data.frame diagnostics one specification. all_diagnostics() : data.frame diagnostics specifications. verif_LY() : Name chosen regression set (possibly without LY). select_reg_one_series() : Name selected regression set.

title: Diagnostics Extraction on Calendar Correction with different sets of regressors — diagnostics_selection
text: get_LY_info() extracts coefficient p-value leap-year (LY) effect. one_diagnostic() applies one X13 specification series computes diagnostics. all_diagnostics() evaluates specifications set summarizes diagnostics. verif_LY() checks whether leap-year effect kept removed. select_reg_one_series() selects best calendar regressors set single series.

title: Diagnostics Extraction on Calendar Correction with different sets of regressors — diagnostics_selection
code: # Create a modelling context my_context <- create_insee_context(s = AirPassengers)  # Generate specification sets my_set <- create_specs_set(context = my_context)  # Extract LY info mod <- rjd3x13::x13(AirPassengers, spec = "RSA3") rjd3production:::get_LY_info(summary(mod)) #>   LY_coeff LY_p_value #> 1       NA         NA  # Compute diagnostics for one spec spec <- my_set[[8L]] rjd3production:::one_diagnostic(series = AirPassengers, spec, context = my_context) #>   note     aicc           mode   LY_coeff  LY_p_value #> 1    0 965.5798 Multiplicative 0.04282008 0.004105741  # Compute diagnostics for all specs rjd3production:::all_diagnostics(series = AirPassengers, specs_set = my_set, context = my_context) #> Computing spec No_TD ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG1 ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG2 ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG3 ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG5 ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG6 ...Done ! #> Computing spec LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG1_LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG2_LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG3_LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG5_LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG6_LY ...Done ! #>            regs note     aicc           mode     LY_coeff   LY_p_value #> No_TD     No_TD    3 987.3845 Multiplicative           NA           NA #> REG1       REG1    0 971.5899 Multiplicative -0.002719545 1.511018e-05 #> REG2       REG2    0 973.7502 Multiplicative           NA           NA #> REG3       REG3    0 975.7970 Multiplicative           NA           NA #> REG5       REG5    0 951.3999 Multiplicative           NA           NA #> REG6       REG6    0 980.0282 Multiplicative           NA           NA #> LY           LY    3 984.0518 Multiplicative  0.039174106 1.941394e-02 #> REG1_LY REG1_LY    0 965.5798 Multiplicative  0.042820081 4.105741e-03 #> REG2_LY REG2_LY    0 952.8547 Multiplicative  0.045206262 6.698235e-04 #> REG3_LY REG3_LY    0 969.7974 Multiplicative  0.042993814 4.285800e-03 #> REG5_LY REG5_LY    0 971.7842 Multiplicative  0.044359491 3.809563e-03 #> REG6_LY REG6_LY    0 974.0675 Multiplicative  0.043817320 4.819346e-03  # Check whether LY should be removed diags <- rjd3production:::all_diagnostics(     series = AirPassengers,     specs_set = my_set,     context = my_context ) #> Computing spec No_TD ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG1 ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG2 ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG3 ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG5 ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG6 ...Done ! #> Computing spec LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG1_LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG2_LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG3_LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG5_LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG6_LY ...Done ! rjd3production:::verif_LY("REG6_LY", diags) #> [1] "REG6"  # Select regressions for one series rjd3production:::select_reg_one_series(series = AirPassengers, context = my_context) #> Computing spec No_TD ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG1 ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG2 ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG3 ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG5 ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG6 ...Done ! #> Computing spec LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG1_LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG2_LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG3_LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG5_LY ...Done ! #> Computing spec REG6_LY ...Done ! #> [1] "REG5"

what: Retrieve a SA-Item by its name — get_jsai_by_name
title: Retrieve a SA-Item by its name — get_jsai_by_name
text: Searches workspace seasonal adjustment item (SAI) whose name matches user-supplied string returns corresponding object.

title: Retrieve a SA-Item by its name — get_jsai_by_name
code: get_jsai_by_name(jws, series_name)

title: Retrieve a SA-Item by its name — get_jsai_by_name
text: jws Java Workspace object, returned rjd3workspace::jws_open() rjd3workspace::jws_new(). series_name character Name SAI retrieve.

title: Retrieve a SA-Item by its name — get_jsai_by_name
text: Java Seasonal Adjustment Item object (jsai).

title: Retrieve a SA-Item by its name — get_jsai_by_name
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ path <- file.path(tempdir(), "workspace_RSA3.xml") jws <- jws_open(path) jws_compute(jws)  jsai <- get_jsai_by_name(jws, "series_1") df <- get_series(jsai) head(df) } # }

what: Extract all series from a SA-Item — get_series
title: Extract all series from a SA-Item — get_series
text: Extracts available time series (pre-adjustment, decomposition, final) seasonal adjustment item (jsai) inside JDemetra+ workspace.

title: Extract all series from a SA-Item — get_series
code: get_series(jsai)

title: Extract all series from a SA-Item — get_series
text: jsai Java Seasonal Adjustment Item object, typically obtained via rjd3workspace::jsap_sai() opening computing workspace rjd3workspace::jws_open() rjd3workspace::jws_compute().

title: Extract all series from a SA-Item — get_series
text: data.frame columns: SAI: name SAI, series: type series (e.g. "y", "sa", "trend"), date: observation dates, value: numeric values series.

title: Extract all series from a SA-Item — get_series
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library("rjd3workspace") path <- file.path(tempdir(), "workspace_RSA3.xml") jws <- jws_open(path) jws_compute(jws) jsap <- jws_sap(jws, 1L) jsai <- jsap_sai(jsap, 1L)  df <- get_series(jsai) head(df) } # }

what: INSEE Regressors and Modelling Context — insee_modelling
title: INSEE Regressors and Modelling Context — insee_modelling
text: functions allow construct standard regressors modelling context used INSEE seasonal adjustment: create_french_calendar() creates French national calendar. create_insee_regressors() generates trading day regressors leap-year effect (LY). create_insee_regressors_sets() organizes regressors standard sets (REG1, REG2, …, REG6, without LY). create_insee_context() combines regressors calendar modelling_context object can used directly rjd3toolkit.

title: INSEE Regressors and Modelling Context — insee_modelling
code: create_french_calendar()  create_insee_regressors(   start = c(1990L, 1L),   frequency = 12L,   length = 492L,   s = NULL,   cal = NULL )  create_insee_regressors_sets(   start = c(1990L, 1L),   frequency = 12L,   length = 492L,   s = NULL,   cal = NULL )  create_insee_context(   start = c(1990L, 1L),   frequency = 12L,   length = 492L,   s = NULL )

title: INSEE Regressors and Modelling Context — insee_modelling
text: start [integer vector] Start period format c(year, month) (default c(1990, 1)). frequency integer Series frequency (default 12L). length integer Series length (default 492L). s [numeric NULL] Optional argument adjustment (passed rjd3toolkit). cal calendar class JD3_CALENDAR.

title: INSEE Regressors and Modelling Context — insee_modelling
text: create_french_calendar() returns national_calendar object. create_insee_regressors() returns matrix regressors (working days + LY). create_insee_regressors_sets() returns list regressor sets (REG1, REG2, …, REG6, without LY). create_insee_context() returns modelling_context object.

title: INSEE Regressors and Modelling Context — insee_modelling

code: jws <- jws_new() #> Error in jws_new(): could not find function "jws_new" jsap <- jws_sap_new(jws, "sap1") #> Error in jws_sap_new(jws, "sap1"): could not find function "jws_sap_new" add_sa_item(     jsap = jsap,     name = "series_3",     x = AirPassengers,     spec = rjd3x13::x13_spec("RSA3") ) #> Error in add_sa_item(jsap = jsap, name = "series_3", x = AirPassengers,     spec = rjd3x13::x13_spec("RSA3")): could not find function "add_sa_item" jws <- make_ws_crunchable(jws) #> Error: object 'jws' not found

what: Manage outliers from JDemetra+ workspaces — export_outliers
title: Manage outliers from JDemetra+ workspaces — export_outliers
text: functions allow extracting, exporting importing outliers detected JDemetra+ workspace: retrieve_outliers() extracts outliers .xml workspace. export_outliers() saves extracted outliers YAML file. import_outliers() loads outliers back .xml workspace YAML file. useful archiving reusing outliers detected seasonal adjustment workflows.

title: Manage outliers from JDemetra+ workspaces — export_outliers
code: export_outliers(x, ws_name, path = NULL, verbose = TRUE)  import_outliers(ws_name, path = NULL, verbose = TRUE)  retrieve_outliers(   jws,   domain = TRUE,   estimation = FALSE,   point = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Manage outliers from JDemetra+ workspaces — export_outliers
text: x list list outliers, returned retrieve_outliers(). ws_name [character] name workspace, used build default YAML filenames. path [character] Path YAML file write read . missing, defaults "regression/outliers_<ws_name>.yaml". verbose [logical] Whether print informative messages (default: TRUE). jws Java Workspace object, returned rjd3workspace::jws_open() rjd3workspace::jws_new(). domain Boolean indicating outliers extracted domain specification. estimation Boolean indicating outliers extracted estimation specification. point Boolean indicating outliers extracted point specification.

title: Manage outliers from JDemetra+ workspaces — export_outliers
text: retrieve_outliers() returns named list element corresponds series workspace contains names existing (detected pre-specified) outliers. export_outliers() invisibly returns path YAML file written. import_outliers() returns list outliers read YAML file.

title: Manage outliers from JDemetra+ workspaces — export_outliers
code: library("rjd3workspace") # Load a Workspace file <- system.file("workspaces", "workspace_test.xml", package = "rjd3workspace") jws <- jws_open(file)  my_dir <- tempdir()  outliers <- retrieve_outliers(jws, point = TRUE, domain = FALSE, estimation = FALSE) #> Série RF0812, 1/5 #> Série RF0893, 2/5 #> Série RF0899, 3/5 #> Série RF1011, 4/5 #> Série RF1012, 5/5 export_outliers(outliers, ws_name = "workspace1", path = my_dir) #> The outliers will be written at  /tmp/Rtmp0Im9RB/outliers_workspace1.yaml  imported <- import_outliers(ws_name = "workspace1", path = my_dir) #> The outliers will be read at  /tmp/Rtmp0Im9RB/outliers_workspace1.yaml

what: Remove non-significant outliers from a JDemetra+ workspace — remove_non_significative_outliers
title: Remove non-significant outliers from a JDemetra+ workspace — remove_non_significative_outliers
text: function scans JDemetra+ workspace (.xml) removes regression outliers whose p-values given threshold. estimation specification domain specification updated accordingly, workspace file saved place. Typical use case: estimation user pre-specified outliers, outliers weak statistical significance (e.g. p > 0.3) dropped simplify regression specification.

title: Remove non-significant outliers from a JDemetra+ workspace — remove_non_significative_outliers
code: remove_non_significative_outliers(   ws_path,   threshold = 0.3,   domain = FALSE,   estimation = FALSE )

title: Remove non-significant outliers from a JDemetra+ workspace — remove_non_significative_outliers
text: ws_path [character] Path JDemetra+ workspace file (usually extension .xml). threshold [numeric] Maximum p-value keeping outlier. Outliers Pr(>|t|) > threshold removed. Default 0.3. domain Boolean indicating domain specification modified. estimation Boolean indicating estimation specification modified.

title: Remove non-significant outliers from a JDemetra+ workspace — remove_non_significative_outliers
text: function invisibly returns NULL, modifies workspace file place (saved location ws_path).

title: Remove non-significant outliers from a JDemetra+ workspace — remove_non_significative_outliers
text: function: iterates series (SA-Items) workspace, identifies outliers regarima specification, checks p-values pre-processing regression summary, removes p-values threshold estimationSpec , present, domainSpec, saves workspace file.

title: Remove non-significant outliers from a JDemetra+ workspace — remove_non_significative_outliers
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Remove non-significant outliers (p > 0.3) from a workspace remove_non_significative_outliers("workspace.xml", threshold = 0.3) } # }

what: Select Calendar Regressors for One or Multiple Series — select_regs
title: Select Calendar Regressors for One or Multiple Series — select_regs
text: Applies X13 regression selection procedure one time series. multiple series provided columns matrix data.frame, series processed separately. function returns selected set regressors series.

title: Select Calendar Regressors for One or Multiple Series — select_regs
code: select_regs(series, context = NULL, ...)

title: Select Calendar Regressors for One or Multiple Series — select_regs
text: series [ts mts matrix data.frame] univariate time series (ts) multivariate series (columns separate series). context list Modeling context created rjd3toolkit::modelling_context(). ... Additional arguments passed create_specs_set() controlling generation X13 specifications. Possible arguments include: outliers Optional list outliers elements type (vector types, e.g., "AO", "LS", "TC") date (vector dates). span_start Starting date estimation (character, format "YYYY-MM-DD"). ... arguments accepted create_specs_set().

title: Select Calendar Regressors for One or Multiple Series — select_regs
text: data.frame two columns: series Name series (column name series multivariate). reg_selected Name selected regressor set.

title: Select Calendar Regressors for One or Multiple Series — select_regs

what: Set span minimum to a value — set_minimum_span
title: Set span minimum to a value — set_minimum_span
text: Set span minimum value

title: Set span minimum to a value — set_minimum_span
code: set_minimum_span(   spec,   d0,   model_span = TRUE,   series_span = TRUE,   without_outliers = TRUE )

title: Set span minimum to a value — set_minimum_span
text: spec Specification (object class JD3_X13_SPEC JD3_TRAMOSEATS_SPEC d0 characters format "YYYY-MM-DD" specify first date span model_span Boolean. estimation (= model) span modifed? series_span Boolean. series (= basic) span modifed? without_outliers Boolean. outliers set starting date removed? (Small crutch waiting resolution jdemetra/jdplus-main issue 858.)

title: Set span minimum to a value — set_minimum_span
text: model_span = estimation_span series_span = basic_span

what: Manage trading-day regressors (TD) from JDemetra+ workspaces — export_td
title: Manage trading-day regressors (TD) from JDemetra+ workspaces — export_td
text: functions allow extract, export import calendar (TD) regressors used JDemetra+ workspaces: retrieve_td() extracts TD specification .xml workspace. export_td() saves extracted TD information YAML file. import_td() loads TD information back YAML file. useful documenting reusing regression settings applied seasonal adjustment workflows.

title: Manage trading-day regressors (TD) from JDemetra+ workspaces — export_td
code: export_td(x, ws_name, path = NULL, verbose = TRUE)  import_td(ws_name, path = NULL, verbose = TRUE)  retrieve_td(   jws,   domain = TRUE,   estimation = FALSE,   point = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Manage trading-day regressors (TD) from JDemetra+ workspaces — export_td
text: x [list | data.frame] object containing TD information, typically output retrieve_td(). ws_name [character] name workspace, used build default YAML file names. path [character] Path YAML file write read . missing, defaults "regression/td_<ws_name>.yaml". verbose [logical] Whether print informative messages (default: TRUE). jws Java Workspace object, returned rjd3workspace::jws_open() rjd3workspace::jws_new(). domain Boolean indicating outliers extracted domain specification. estimation Boolean indicating outliers extracted estimation specification. point Boolean indicating outliers extracted point specification.

title: Manage trading-day regressors (TD) from JDemetra+ workspaces — export_td
text: retrieve_td() returns data.frame columns: series: series names, regs: TD regressor specification (REG1, REG2, …, REG6, without _LY), export_td() invisibly returns path YAML file written. import_td() returns list data structure read YAML.

title: Manage trading-day regressors (TD) from JDemetra+ workspaces — export_td
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ws_file <- "path/to/workspace.xml"  # 1. Retrieve TD specification td <- retrieve_td(ws_file)  # 2. Export to YAML export_td(td, ws_name = "workspace1")  # 3. Import back from YAML imported <- import_td(x = NULL, ws_name = "workspace1") } # }

title: rjd3production 1.0.0.9000
